thesis_status_reasoning: The architecture is experiencing simultaneous stress across multiple interconnected systems. AFN par deviation (-$0.58) has entered elevated range, High-Yield Treasury Facility is experiencing significant net outflows ($1.75B this period, exceeding the $500M elevated threshold), and ASE has declined 11.8% this period (approaching the 15% elevated threshold). However, the swap mechanism is functioning—redemptions cleared with only 1.4% ASE supply growth, well below warning levels—and the AFN recovered from worse deviations. This represents a stress test in progress, not yet a structural failure, but the Compound Stress Matrix shows two of three indicators in elevated range simultaneously.
action_reasoning: The system has entered the ELEVATED tier of the Compound Stress Matrix (AFN deviation > $0.50 AND Facility net outflows > $500M). While no kill switches are triggered, the pre-loaded structure is now more vulnerable to cascading failure. Critical next observation: whether Facility outflows accelerate, ASE decline continues toward 15%/period threshold, or AFN deviation widens toward $2.00.

key_signals.signal: High-Yield Treasury Facility net outflows of $1.75B this period, reducing deposits from implied ~$18.85B to $17.1B, concentration ratio unchanged at 73.1% suggesting proportional withdrawals across depositor base
AFN deviation of -$0.58 from par, within normal band but at elevated threshold, with $650M redemptions this period representing 2.8% of outstanding supply
Debt-to-equity swap mechanism cleared $650M redemptions with only 1.4% ASE supply growth, demonstrating functional conversion without hyperinflation of equity supply
ASE price decline of 11.8% this period ($97.50, down $13), approaching but not yet reaching the 15% elevated threshold for compound stress
Stabilization Consortium coverage ratio at 17.8%, below the 20% WARNING threshold but above the 15% TRIGGERED threshold, with collateral experiencing mark-to-market losses
Posted yield of 24.2% vs estimated organic yield of 7.4% creates a 16.8 percentage point gap (227% of organic yield), far exceeding the 10% WARNING threshold for yield sustainability
Analyst consensus describes this as 'temporary volatility' with 'architecture held under stress test' and par recovery emphasized, suggesting institutional confidence has not collapsed
Consortium last collateral acquisition was Day -12, with current status 'monitoring' rather than 'deploying', suggesting they are not yet intervening despite reserve ratio warning breach

biggest_uncertainty: Whether the $1.75B Facility outflows represent rational portfolio rebalancing by sophisticated institutions who remain committed to the architecture, or the beginning of mercenary capital flight that will accelerate if ASE continues declining and AFN deviation widens—the distinction determines if this is a survivable stress test or the start of a death spiral
